name: Ribamontán Circuit
name_original: Somo, Villaverde de Pontones, Beranga, San Miguel de Meruelo – ruta circular
region: Lic-Dunas Del Puntal Y Estuario Del Miera
description: This 33.5-mile moderate road cycling route through Cantabria offers coastal views, charming villages, and 1988 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes in Lic-Dunas Del Puntal Y Estuario Del Miera traverse a dynamic landscape characterized by coastal spits, extensive dune systems, and the Miera River estuary. The terrain offers a mix of flat coastal sections and moderate elevation gains, providing varied experiences for road cyclists. This region features a distinctive mosaic of habitats, including the El Puntal-Somo-Loredo coastal spit and the islands of Santa Marina and Mouro. The area's road cycling terrain and elevation are shaped by its estuarine and coastal environments, with well-maintained paths connecting key natural features. Best road cycling routes in Lic-Dunas Del Puntal Y Estuario Del Miera The most popular road cycling route is Ribamontán Circuit, a 33.5 miles (53.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route features moderate elevation changes through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Alisas – Somo Beach loop from Somo, a moderate 35.9 miles (57.7 km) path. This route offers scenic views of the coast and includes significant climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Somo Beach – View of the Estuary loop from Somo, a 34.3 miles (55.3 km) trail leading through coastal areas and offering views of the estuary, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Road cycling in Lic-Dunas Del Puntal Y Estuario Del Miera is defined by coastal paths, estuarine views, and varied terrain with moderate elevation gains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier coastal rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Lic-Dunas Del Puntal Y Estuario Del Miera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 7,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lic-Dunas Del Puntal Y Estuario Del Miera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Lic-Dunas Del Puntal Y Estuario Del Miera?
A: There are over 160 road cycling routes in Lic-Dunas Del Puntal Y Estuario Del Miera, offering a wide range of options for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on road cycling routes in this region?
A: The region offers a dynamic landscape with a mix of flat coastal sections and moderate elevation gains. You'll find routes traversing coastal spits, extensive dune systems, and the Miera River estuary, providing varied experiences from easier coastal rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 29 easy road cycling routes available, which are generally suitable for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes often feature well-maintained paths and flatter sections, particularly along the coast.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: You can explore a distinctive mosaic of habitats, including the extensive dune system of El Puntal and the Miera River estuary. Routes often offer scenic coastal views and pass by significant natural attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4967873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somo Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5582331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Los Tranquilos&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1121761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs and Beach Trail Facing the Bay of Santander&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Lic-Dunas Del Puntal Y Estuario Del Miera?
A: The region is accessible and enjoyable for cycling throughout the year. However, the spring and autumn months typically offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal for exploring the coastal and estuarine landscapes. Summer is also popular, though it can be busier.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling here?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 150 reviews. Cyclists often praise the picturesque coastal views, the varied terrain that caters to different abilities, and the unique blend of estuarine and dune environments.

Q: Can you recommend a challenging road cycling route with significant climbs?
A: For a more challenging ride with significant climbs and scenic coastal views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39552366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Alisas – Somo Beach loop from Somo&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers 35.9 miles (57.7 km) and includes notable ascents.

Q: Are there any loop routes for road cycling?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005125389/ribamontan-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribamontán Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is a popular loop covering 33.5 miles (53.9 km) with moderate elevation changes.

Q: What are some options for road cycling routes that offer estuary views?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24719604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somo Beach – View of the Estuary loop from Somo&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for experiencing the Miera River estuary. This 34.3-mile (55.3 km) trail leads through coastal areas and provides beautiful views of the estuary.

Q: Are there facilities or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: While specific cafes or pubs are not detailed for every route, the region's accessibility and proximity to urban centers mean you'll find various amenities in towns and villages along or near the cycling paths, especially around areas like Somo and Loredo.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in the area?
A: One of the longer and more challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somo Beach – Puerto de Alisas loop from Somo&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 36.8 miles (59.3 km) and features significant elevation gain, making it a difficult ride.
tips: Somo beach is undoubtedly one of the best Spanish and European surfing spots. There are waves almost every day of the year, there are plenty of schools and the atmosphere is very fun. Somo beach is located between Puntal and Loredo beach, another mythical surfing spot in the north of Spain.

Section between green meadows with a parallel bike lane separated from the road. From the bicycle you can quietly enjoy the passage through this typical area of pastures and see some herds of cows or sheep.
The 'old fountain' and typical old laundry.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 53.9 km (33.5 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours and 32 minutes to complete on a race bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, characteristic of Cantabria's diverse landscape. You can expect a mix of coastal roads with gentle gradients, verdant meadows, and rolling hills. Inland sections might present more challenging ascents and descents, though the route is generally considered moderate.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning and constantly changing scenery. You'll experience picturesque views of the Cantabrian Sea, beautiful beaches, and dramatic cliffs along the coastal stretches. Inland, you'll be immersed in lush green forests and charming, traditional Cantabrian villages, showcasing the region's natural and cultural beauty.

faqs.question: What are some notable points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by several interesting spots. Near the start, you can enjoy Somo Beach and a dedicated cycle way near Somo. Further along, you might encounter the historic El Alvareo Fountain and Washhouse, offering a glimpse into local heritage.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. Notably, it follows sections of the Northern Route (Cantabria) for about 13% of its length and the Senda del Litoral for 6%. You'll also encounter smaller local paths like Barrio La Barquería and Paseo Marítimo del Muelle.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in Cantabria is generally during the spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, with less rain and fewer crowds than in peak summer. Summer can also be good, but it might be warmer and busier, especially in coastal areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this route?
faqs.answer: Given that this is a circular route connecting several towns, you have flexibility. Somo, Villaverde de Pontones, Beranga, or San Miguel de Meruelo all offer potential starting points with parking options. Somo, being a coastal town, often has public parking available, though it can get busy during peak season.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, primarily on paved surfaces and public roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not designed as a specific dog-walking trail. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always adhere to local leash laws and clean-up regulations, especially when passing through villages or stopping at beaches.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route or the natural park it passes through?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route. The route passes through the general area of the Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel, but there are no specific entry fees or cycling permits required for public roads within the park's vicinity. Always respect local regulations and protected areas.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects several villages and towns like Somo, Villaverde de Pontones, Beranga, and San Miguel de Meruelo, you'll find opportunities for refreshments and accommodation. Somo, in particular, offers various cafes, restaurants, and hotels. Smaller villages may have local bars or eateries, perfect for a quick stop.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' and involves a significant distance (53.9 km) and some elevation gain. While parts of it might be suitable, the entire circular route is generally better suited for cyclists with a good level of fitness and experience, rather than young children or beginners. Consider shorter, flatter sections if cycling with a family.
